① Signal
Signal
Play the over 5.5.2 units
The Read
✓Stuff — 27% whiff, curveball 32% putaway, 10.1 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
✓Command — 67% FPS, 2.1 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
→Matchup — MIL K-rate runs +3% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #11/30 vs RHP); whiffs 31% on his curveball — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 6.0 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~4.9 IP at his 10.1 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 7.5K vs 5.5 (+2.0K), σ2.3, CAP 71%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 5.5 alt line, not the 6.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: High-confidence edge — 71% model conviction at 7.5K vs 5.5 line.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.9 IP to hit 5.5 at his 10.1 K/9 — his 6.0 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓MIL whiffs 31% on his curveball — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CU finishes counts (32% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Start-to-start variance (σ2.3K) — the projection is a median, not a floor

① Signal
Signal
Over 3.5 is the play.1.5 units
The Read
→Stuff — 23% whiff, slider 26% putaway, 7.2 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 66% FPS, 3.5 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
⚠Matchup — KC K-rate runs -3% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #12/30 vs RHP); whiffs 32% on his slider — a mild headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 5.7 IP avg — clears 3.5 with ~4.4 IP at his 7.2 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 4.6K vs 3.5 (+1.1K), σ1.7, CAP 65%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 3.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 4.6K vs 3.5. 65% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.4 IP to hit 3.5 at his 7.2 K/9 — his 5.7 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓KC whiffs 32% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SL finishes counts (26% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Start-to-start variance (σ1.7K) — the projection is a median, not a floor

① Signal
Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
→Stuff — 24% whiff, curveball 27% putaway, 7.5 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
✓Command — 67% FPS, 2.4 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
✓Matchup — LAA K-rate runs +14% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #29/30 vs RHP); whiffs 36% on his curveball — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
✓Volume — 6.1 IP avg — pitches deep; clears 4.5 with ~5.4 IP at his 7.5 K/9 pace, so there's room to spare.
✓Projection — 5.6K vs 4.5 (+1.1K), σ1.4, CAP 64%, 10 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 5.6K vs 4.5. 64% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓LAA whiffs 36% on his curveball — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CU finishes counts (27% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓LAA K-rate runs +14% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.7K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.4 IP to reach 4.5, right at his 6.1 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

① Signal
Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 27% whiff, curveball 29% putaway, 9.2 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
→Command — 65% FPS, 2.8 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — DET K-rate runs +4% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #22/30 vs RHP); whiffs 35% on his curveball — a mild tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
→Volume — 5.0 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.4 IP at his 9.2 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 5.6K vs 4.5 (+1.1K), σ1.7, CAP 64%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 5.6K vs 4.5. 64% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓DET whiffs 35% on his curveball — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CU finishes counts (29% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~4.4 IP to reach 4.5, right at his 5.0 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

→Volume — 5.3 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~5.1 IP at his 9.7 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.

① Signal
Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 27% whiff, sweeper 32% putaway, 10.1 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
→Command — 59% FPS, 2.6 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
⚠Matchup — WSH K-rate runs -9% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #18/30 vs LHP); whiffs 30% on his sweeper — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 5.4 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.0 IP at his 10.1 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.5 × oppK 21.1 → −0.61K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.6K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
⚠Projection — 5.6K vs 4.5 (+1.1K), σ2.5, CAP 63%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 5.6K vs 4.5. 63% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.0 IP to hit 4.5 at his 10.1 K/9 — his 5.4 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓WSH whiffs 30% on his sweeper — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SW finishes counts (32% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗WSH K-rate runs -9% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~0.5K

The Read
✓Stuff — 29% whiff, changeup 35% putaway, 10.6 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
✓Command — 64% FPS, 2.3 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
⚠Matchup — STL K-rate runs -18% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #2/30 vs LHP); whiffs 30% on his changeup — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
✓Volume — 6.0 IP avg — pitches deep; clears 5.5 with ~4.7 IP at his 10.6 K/9 pace, so there's room to spare.
⚠Volatility × matchup — σ2.1 × oppK 18.8 → −0.29K — a volatile K profile against a contact-leaning lineup. This exact combo historically over-projects (−1.5K bias in that cell), so the projection is trimmed 0.3K — already reflected in the PROJ, edge and tier above.
✓Projection — 6.6K vs 5.5 (+1.1K), σ2.1, CAP 62%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 6.6K vs 5.5. 62% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.7 IP to hit 5.5 at his 10.6 K/9 — his 6.0 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓STL whiffs 30% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (35% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗STL K-rate runs -18% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~1.3K

The Read
✓Stuff — 30% whiff, splitter 36% putaway, 8.7 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
→Command — 60% FPS, 2.3 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
✓Matchup — CIN K-rate runs +22% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #25/30 vs LHP); whiffs 58% on his splitter — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
⚠Volume — 5.6 IP avg — short outings; clears 5.5 with ~5.7 IP at his 8.7 K/9 pace, so he must go deeper than usual.
✓Projection — 6.5K vs 5.5 (+1.0K), σ2.0, CAP 61%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 6.5K vs 5.5. 61% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.

✓CIN whiffs 58% on his splitter — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓FS finishes counts (36% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓2.3 BB/9 — no free passes; he attacks and every PA is a K chance
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.7 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 5.6 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

The Read
→Stuff — 26% whiff, curveball 29% putaway, 9.6 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
⚠Command — 4.2 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
✓Matchup — PIT K-rate runs +8% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #24/30 vs RHP); whiffs 33% on his curveball — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
→Volume — 4.8 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.2 IP at his 9.6 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 5.4K vs 4.5 (+0.9K), σ2.2, CAP 60%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 5.4K vs 4.5. 60% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓PIT whiffs 33% on his curveball — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CU finishes counts (29% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓PIT K-rate runs +8% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.4K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~4.2 IP to reach 4.5, right at his 4.8 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗4.2 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash

The Read
⚠Stuff — 22% whiff, changeup 26% putaway, 6.7 K/9 — below-average whiff; needs the matchup to carry the Ks.
✓Command — 65% FPS, 2.4 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
→Matchup — CLE K-rate runs +0% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #18/30 vs RHP); whiffs 31% on his changeup — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
✓Volume — 6.6 IP avg — pitches deep; clears 4.5 with ~6.0 IP at his 6.7 K/9 pace, so there's room to spare.
✓Projection — 5.3K vs 4.5 (+0.8K), σ1.7, CAP 60%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 4.5 alt line, not the 5.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 5.3K vs 4.5. 60% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓CLE whiffs 31% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (26% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if

▲ OVER · 1u
CAP 59% · needs 59.7% (-148) · model 60% (-153) · +0.8% edge
L3.5
-148
① Signal
Signal
Over 3.5 is the play.1 unit
The Read
→Stuff — 22% whiff, changeup 26% putaway, 7.6 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 59% FPS, 3.4 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
⚠Matchup — ATL K-rate runs -5% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #7/30 vs RHP); whiffs 27% on his changeup — a mild headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 5.2 IP avg — clears 3.5 with ~4.1 IP at his 7.6 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 4.2K vs 3.5 (+0.7K), σ2.1, CAP 59%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Solid over edge at 4.2K vs 3.5. 59% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~4.1 IP to hit 3.5 at his 7.6 K/9 — his 5.2 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓ATL whiffs 27% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (26% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗ATL K-rate runs -5% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~0.2K

L5.5
-128
① Signal
Signal
Lean over 5.5 — size down.0.5 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 26% whiff, curveball 31% putaway, 10.8 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
⚠Command — 4.4 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
→Matchup — PHI K-rate runs +2% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #21/30 vs RHP); whiffs 28% on his curveball — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 4.7 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~4.6 IP at his 10.8 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
⚠Projection — 6.2K vs 5.5 (+0.7K), σ2.9, CAP 57%, 10 starts — real edge, but the floor is live (high variance).
Verdict: Marginal over edge (6.2K vs 5.5), 57% conviction — take it reduced.
Bet wins if
✓PHI whiffs 28% on his curveball — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CU finishes counts (31% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~4.6 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 4.7 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗4.4 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash
✗σ2.9K — floor sits near 3K, under 5.5 on an off night

① Signal
Signal
Lean over 5.5 — size down.0.5 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 30% whiff, curveball 35% putaway, 10.0 K/9 — genuine bat-misser who finishes counts; the K ceiling is real.
⚠Command — 55% FPS — falls behind too often; weak contact tends to follow, not Ks.
⚠Matchup — AZ K-rate runs -16% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #4/30 vs RHP); whiffs 21% on his curveball — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
✓Volume — 6.2 IP avg — pitches deep; clears 5.5 with ~5.0 IP at his 10.0 K/9 pace, so there's room to spare.
✓Projection — 6.2K vs 5.5 (+0.7K), σ1.6, CAP 57%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 5.5 alt line, not the 6.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Marginal over edge (6.2K vs 5.5), 57% conviction — take it reduced.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~5.0 IP to hit 5.5 at his 10.0 K/9 — his 6.2 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓CU finishes counts (35% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗AZ K-rate runs -16% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~1.1K

▲ OVER · 0.5u
CAP 57% · needs 68.3% (-215) · model 59% (-142) · -9.7% edge
L3.5
-215
① Signal
Signal
Lean over 3.5 — size down.0.5 unit
The Read
→Stuff — 24% whiff, slider 29% putaway, 8.2 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
⚠Command — 5.4 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
✓Matchup — BAL K-rate runs +12% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #27/30 vs RHP); whiffs 35% on his slider — a strong tailwind; more K-prone than average vs his specific mix, the number is beatable on matchup.
→Volume — 4.2 IP avg — clears 3.5 with ~3.8 IP at his 8.2 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 4.1K vs 3.5 (+0.6K), σ1.7, CAP 57%, 8 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 3.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Marginal over edge (4.1K vs 3.5), 57% conviction — take it reduced.
Bet wins if
✓BAL whiffs 35% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SL finishes counts (29% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓BAL K-rate runs +12% vs neutral for his mix — more K-prone than average, worth ~0.4K over a neutral draw
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~3.8 IP to reach 3.5, right at his 4.2 IP avg — one short outing and it misses
✗5.4 BB/9 — walks burn pitch count and cut his effective innings before any leash

CAP 74% · needs 59.3% (-146) · model 78% (-357) · +18.8% edge
L4.5
-146
① Signal
Signal
Under 4.5 is the play.1.5 units
The Read
✓Stuff — 18% whiff, changeup 22% putaway, 5.5 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
✓Command — 64% FPS, 1.6 BB/9 — pounds the zone early; batters put it in play before two-strike counts.
→Matchup — SEA K-rate runs +4% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #23/30 vs RHP); whiffs 33% on his changeup — roughly neutral K environment for his mix.
✓Volume — 5.8 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 3.2K vs 4.5 (-1.3K), σ1.6, CAP 74%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Strong under signal — 78% model probability below 4.5 at 3.2K projected.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~7.4 IP to reach 4.5 — beyond his 5.8 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓5.5 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 4.5
✓18% whiff on a changeup-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗5.5 K/9 is enough to reach 4.5 in a long outing

✓Matchup — LAD K-rate runs -8% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #7/30 vs LHP); whiffs 29% on his changeup — a strong K-suppressing lineup for his arsenal; reinforces the under.
✓Volume — 6.0 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 3.5K vs 4.5 (-1.0K), σ1.5, CAP 70%, 10 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.

① Signal
Signal
Under 5.5 is the play.1.5 units
The Read
→Stuff — 25% whiff, slider 30% putaway, 7.6 K/9 — average whiff; a K ceiling exists but isn't his identity.
⚠Command — 4.1 BB/9 — walks stretch innings and PAs, which can feed late Ks against the under.
⚠Matchup — COL K-rate runs +7% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #27/30 vs LHP); whiffs 38% on his slider — a mild tailwind; this lineup could push him toward the number.
✓Volume — 5.6 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 4.4K vs 5.5 (-1.1K), σ1.9, CAP 68%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Strong under signal — 72% model probability below 5.5 at 4.4K projected.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~6.5 IP to reach 5.5 — beyond his 5.6 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓5.6 IP avg — modest length caps his total K opportunity
Bet loses if
✗7.6 K/9 — the rate exists to reach 5.5 on a deep outing or if the lineup expands the zone
✗COL K-rate runs +7% vs neutral for his mix — swing-happy enough to feed cheap Ks

① Signal
Signal
Lean under 5.5 — size down.0.5 unit
The Read
✓Stuff — 21% whiff, slider 25% putaway, 8.4 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
⚠Command — 5.3 BB/9 — walks stretch innings and PAs, which can feed late Ks against the under.
✓Matchup — MIN K-rate runs -5% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #9/30 vs RHP); whiffs 35% on his slider — a mild K-suppressing lineup for his arsenal; reinforces the under.
✓Volume — 4.3 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 3.7K vs 5.5 (-1.8K), σ1.6, CAP 76%, 6 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
→Line — best value at the 5.5 alt line, not the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Verdict: Under edge present (3.7K vs 5.5), 76% confidence — lean only.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~5.9 IP to reach 5.5 — beyond his 4.3 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓MIN K-rate runs -5% vs neutral for his mix — a naturally K-suppressing lineup
✓4.3 IP avg — modest length caps his total K opportunity
Bet loses if
✗8.4 K/9 — the rate exists to reach 5.5 on a deep outing or if the lineup expands the zone

CAP 58% · needs 59.3% (-146) · model 60% (-153) · +1.2% edge
L3.5
-146
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
→Stuff — 24% whiff, slider 30% putaway, 9.1 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 62% FPS, 2.0 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
⚠Matchup — SF K-rate runs -9% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #3/30 vs RHP); whiffs 31% on his slider — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
⚠Volume / leash — 4 starts, 4.5 IP avg — injury-return leash is a hard cap on K opportunity. The model already uses the short IP, but an early hook is the main way this loses and a thin sample understates his true variance.
⚠Projection — 4.2K vs 3.5 (+0.7K), σ1.9, CAP 58%, 4 starts — edge is large but σ/CAP on a thin sample read optimistic.
Verdict: Model projects 4.2K vs 3.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Only needs ~3.5 IP to hit 3.5 at his 9.1 K/9 — his 4.5 IP avg is a full inning of cushion
✓SF whiffs 31% on his slider — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SL finishes counts (30% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Injury-return leash — a hook before ~3.5 IP ends the over no matter how he's throwing
✗SF K-rate runs -9% vs neutral for his mix — a contact draw that costs him ~0.4K

L5.5
-132
① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
→Stuff — 23% whiff, sweeper 25% putaway, 8.2 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
✓Command — 66% FPS, 2.3 BB/9 — works ahead and wastes few PAs; every batter becomes a K chance.
→Matchup — NYM K-rate runs +0% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #15/30 vs RHP); whiffs 34% on his sweeper — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
✓Volume — 6.2 IP avg — pitches deep; clears 5.5 with ~6.0 IP at his 8.2 K/9 pace, so there's room to spare.
✓Projection — 5.9K vs 5.5 (+0.4K), σ2.4, CAP 54%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.9K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓NYM whiffs 34% on his sweeper — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓SW finishes counts (25% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
✓2.3 BB/9 — no free passes; he attacks and every PA is a K chance
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~6.0 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 6.2 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

The Read
✓Stuff — 26% whiff, changeup 32% putaway, 9.3 K/9 — solid swing-and-miss; the Ks are there when he pitches ahead.
→Command — 60% FPS, 2.7 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
→Matchup — MIA K-rate runs +2% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #21/30 vs LHP); whiffs 31% on his changeup — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 5.9 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~5.3 IP at his 9.3 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 5.8K vs 5.5 (+0.3K), σ2.2, CAP 52%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.8K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓MIA whiffs 31% on his changeup — his best pitch is exactly what they can't hit
✓CH finishes counts (32% putaway) — two-strike counts convert instead of fouling off
Bet loses if
✗Needs ~5.3 IP to reach 5.5, right at his 5.9 IP avg — one short outing and it misses

L4.5

① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
The Read
✓Stuff — 20% whiff, slider 23% putaway, 7.0 K/9 — below-average swing-and-miss; pitches to contact, not a K arm.
⚠Command — 5.7 BB/9 — walks stretch innings and PAs, which can feed late Ks against the under.
⚠Matchup — CWS K-rate runs +12% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #26/30 vs LHP); whiffs 32% on his slider — a strong tailwind; this lineup could push him toward the number.
✓Volume — 4.5 IP avg — modest length caps total K opportunity; helps the under.
✓Projection — 3.6K vs 4.5 (-0.9K), σ2.0, CAP 29%, 10 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 3.6K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Bet wins if
✓Would need ~5.8 IP to reach 4.5 — beyond his 4.5 IP avg, so length alone won't get there
✓7.0 K/9 — he simply doesn't miss enough bats to threaten 4.5
✓20% whiff on a slider-heavy mix — batters put it in play, few swing-through Ks
Bet loses if
✗CWS K-rate runs +12% vs neutral for his mix — swing-happy enough to feed cheap Ks

→Stuff — 24% whiff, changeup 30% putaway, 8.4 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
→Command — 70% FPS, 2.9 BB/9 — solid count control; supports steady K accumulation.
⚠Matchup — TB K-rate runs -16% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #2/30 vs RHP); whiffs 25% on his changeup — a strong headwind; the edge has to come from his stuff, not this lineup.
→Volume — 5.3 IP avg — clears 4.5 with ~4.8 IP at his 8.4 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 4.6K vs 4.5 (+0.1K), σ1.0, CAP 49%, 10 starts — tight, reliable projection; not one-start noise.
Verdict: Model projects 4.6K vs 4.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

The Read
→Stuff — 25% whiff, curveball 32% putaway, 10.7 K/9 — league-average whiff; leans on sequencing more than raw stuff tonight.
⚠Command — 5.2 BB/9 — free passes eat PAs and pitch count; a real drag on the over.
→Matchup — TEX K-rate runs -1% vs neutral for his specific mix (raw team K% #10/30 vs RHP); whiffs 29% on his curveball — neutral draw vs his mix; neither adds nor subtracts.
→Volume — 4.9 IP avg — clears 5.5 with ~4.6 IP at his 10.7 K/9 pace, right around his workload; efficient but little margin.
✓Projection — 5.7K vs 5.5 (+0.2K), σ2.3, CAP 50%, 6 starts — clean edge at current pricing.
Verdict: Model projects 5.7K vs 5.5 — edge too thin to take a side.

StrategyNW–LWin%ROIΔ

How the flagged bets themselves did (what you’d skip)
FADE4023–1757%+1.2%

ALT41–325%-30.6%
LEASH10–10%-100.0%
THIN10–10%-100.0%
Definitions · HIGH-σ σ ≥ 2.5K (start-to-start K spread) · FADE opp blended K < 21% · K-SUPP opp blended K 21–22.5% · LEASH injury-return or <6 starts & <5.5 IP avg · THIN <8 starts · σ-FADE σ>2 × opp K<23 (proj haircut) · ALT best line ≠ market main

MODEL
signals · methodology · card guide · v9.9
Performance Tracker
through 2026-07-10

Live results by signal tier — updated each session. ROI uses actual FD odds. Bias = avg(actual − projected Ks), positive means model over-projects. MAE = mean absolute projection error in Ks.

TierWin RateROIP&L

■ Win rate bar fills to actual %▲ Bias positive = model tends to over-project Ks◆ MAE in strikeouts — lower is better● Bias within range
Signal Tiers — What Each Badge Means
STRONG · 2uHighest conviction. Model projects meaningfully more Ks than the line with strong probability and high confidence across multiple signals. Recommended 2 unit bet.
MOD · 1uModerate conviction. Clear edge vs the line with reasonable confidence. Standard 1 unit bet (1.5u when confidence is elevated and variance is low).
LEAN O · 0.5uDirectional lean over. Model sees an edge but confidence or probability does not clear the full action threshold. Half unit only.
LEAN U · 0.5uDirectional lean under. Model projects below the line with some confidence but not enough for full conviction. Half unit only.
UNDER · 1uHigh conviction fade. Model projects meaningfully fewer Ks than the line with strong confidence. 1 unit bet (1.5u when confidence is highest).
WATCH ⇑⇓Signal exists but does not clear the action threshold — confidence too low or variance too high. No bet recommended. Orange = over direction, Silver = under. Blocker reason shown on the card.
PASSNo meaningful signal. Model projects close to the line or insufficient data to form a view.
Overlay Flags
LEASH — injury-return / short-leash arm; the over tier is capped one notch (direction holds, size comes down) until he shows 6+ IP
FADE / K-SUPP — contact-oriented lineup that suppresses strikeouts vs this arsenal — caution on overs
HIGH-σ — volatile start-to-start K totals; the projection is a median, not a floor. On its own this is fine — it only bites when paired with a contact lineup (see σ-FADE)
σ-FADE — the volatile-arm-vs-contact-lineup combo. Historically this exact pairing over-projects strikeouts, so the model now trims the projection on a sliding scale (bigger trim the more volatile the arm AND the more contact-oriented the lineup)
THIN — small sample (<8 starts); confidence lower than the number implies
ALT — best value is on an alternate line, not the market main — be on the right number
These are recorded with each bet and shown in the Results tab, so past performance can be sliced by flag.
CAP — Model Confidence

CAP (0-100%) measures how aligned the signals are — not a win probability. High CAP means multiple independent signals point the same direction. Used to gate tier assignment — higher tiers require higher CAP.

Reading a Card
The Read — bulleted matchup analysis: each line is a data point → what it means → direction (✓ supports · ⚠ risk · → neutral), ending in a Verdict tied to sizing
PROJ strip — model projection, edge vs line, P(over), CAP confidence, FD odds on the recommended line
OPP K-MIX strip — three K rates on one comparable scale: BBRef season team K% · MIX-K% (re-weighted for this pitcher's arsenal — can exceed BBRef when they're extra vulnerable to his mix) · Blended (feeds the projection). Green = pitcher-favorable
Arsenal box — per-pitch usage, pitcher whiff%, opponent whiff%, matchup grade. Below it: STUFF (usage-weighted whiff), VS LINEUP (matchup amplifier bar — right/green = lineup amplifies his whiffs), K ENGINE (donut showing which pitches drive his strikeouts vs this lineup), and a recent hit-rate row (dot per last-10 start: green = went over, red = stayed under, with a side-aware tally)
Lineup panel — confirmed order; per batter, per pitch: whiff% (bold) over K% (lighter shade). Green = pitcher advantage, red = contact
Implied Odds box — probability of clearing each alt line as American odds. BET = recommended line, MKT = market main
Signal strip — action, unit size, and verdict
Data Sources
Live each session — pitcher L10 K counts, IP, ERA · FanDuel alt lines and odds · confirmed lineups
Savant CSV — batter K%/whiff%/xBA per pitch type · pitcher arsenal whiff%/FPS%/putaway%
Researched — team wRC+ by handedness · L10 runs/game · park factors · bullpen ERA · BBRef team K% splits
Not included — live Statcast splits · bullpen usage · real-time injury status beyond confirmed lineup
Hits TabBETA

P(≥1 hit) for each batter tonight. Built from confirmed lineups and pitcher arsenal data. Sorted by edge vs FanDuel's posted hit line. Early stage — no bullpen data or recent trends. Large edges often reflect model limitations, not market errors.

⚠ For informational and entertainment purposes only. Projections are model outputs — not guarantees. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Bet responsibly.
